So now I'm going to go as I'm going to go and I'm going to show my stack slide number one. The psychology behind the stack slide, I learned this initially from Armand Moran. It has made me more money collectively probably than anything else I've ever done. The first time you do it, you will feel weird and awkward and embarrassed, but you will get over it when you're cashing checks that are bigger than anything you ever dreamt of. So the psychology behind it, when most people speak that haven't learned this process through me or through Armand, I think the only guys who have ever taught it. But what most people do is they talk about their offer, they talk about the bonuses, they go through all this stuff, and then the last thing they show, they show bonus like number five or six or ten or whatever it is, and then they talk about the price. And the problem is our human minds are very, we can remember things not like, it's very short of our memory. So what happens is like, here's the best thing I got, then bonus number two, number three, number four, and this is my smallest bonus, and then this is the price. And what happens is that your brain associates the price with the last thing it saw. And so if the last thing you offer them isn't worth that much, then they're not going to buy. And so Armand taught me, I was out speaking doing some stuff, and he was speaking on a stage, it was a Dan Kennedy stage, and before he got up he's like, hey man, I want to show you something. Like what's that? He's like, it's called the stack. I'm like, what is it? He's like, just watch. And he got up on stage, and I'm watching Armand, who's like, one of the first guys I learned tons of this speaking from, he goes through, does this whole pitch, and I'm watching and he gets to the stack, and it was like, I didn't know what it was, but it's this weird thing, and it was like super fag, all this stuff was happening, I didn't know what it was, and then he closed, and I saw this room of 1,200 people. I saw more than half of them jump up and run to the back and sign up for his thing, and I'm sitting there back just watching, doing the math, counting everything, and it was done. I waited every kind of left, and he walked over and he had this big old stack, fast stack of order forms this big, and he walked over, and he's like, do you see that? I was like, what was that? He's like, it was the stack. He's like, do you want order forms like this? I'm like, yeah, he's like, then you got to learn the stack. And he kind of walked away, I'm like, dude, what's the stack? He's like, all right. I'll tell you. And he told me what I told you guys about the whole memory thing. He's like, watch what I do. He's like, I have a slide where I talk about my number one thing and I talk about it. And then I stack it, right? So I'll show you the stack slide, which is kind of jacked up because of the keynote thing, so I apologize, it looks ugly, but conceptually, here's the first thing. Then you introduce the second thing, you talk about it, and you come back to the slide, and now you stack the second thing. And the third thing, the fourth thing, and by the time it's done, I have all these things, I stack them all together, and that's my introduced price. Now they're just, they're like, they're rationalizing the price with the entire thing, not just the last thing you talked about. Okay? And so you're going to feel kind of weird. I promise you will feel, do you guys feel weird the first time you did it? It's kind of weird. Yeah, I didn't like it either. In fact, the Armand told me, I was speaking to his event two weeks later. He said, if you speak at my stage, you have to use the stack because I need you to make money. And I was like, all right. So he taught it to me, he sent me his slides, I like learned it, and went to that next event. I did the stack, and I was the number one seller at his event. Made more money on stage in one time than I ever made prior. And it was cool, I don't know if Brent's here, but Brent came to that event with me, and he was sitting there, and we got done, and everything happened. Like I had my big stack of order forms. I think I made, it was like $160,000 in sales, and I got half that. So like 80 grand was mine to take home. He was like, dude, you made 80 grand in 90 minutes. That's insane. I was like, I know dude, it was the stack. Like this is magic. And ever since then, I've never not done, actually it's not true. One time, I like, I'd done the stack a bunch of times it worked. And one time, I was like, kind of embarrassed. I'm like, ah, it's not going to work. I'm just going to, and I didn't do it. And I blanked on stage. It was the most embarrassing thing in my life. And I swore that day I will never, ever give a presentation, I don't do the stack again. So like it literally, everything I've ever done, like I can contribute more money to this one concept than anything else I've done. So like just use it. It'll be for weird the first time, and by the second or third time, you'll love it, and then you'll never want to leave it away. Because it's just, it's so worth it. Okay, so talk about my master class, and I stack it. That means you're going to get a six week funnel-hacks master class, total value of $2,997. Okay, and then I introduce now the tool.
